products.name: Unified Agent Runtime
products.description: The canonical runtime for Rootz AI agents. Provides AgentEvent type system (25 categories), InternalChain (Merkle-linked blocks), SkillRegistry (manifest validation), and Zod schemas for runtime validation. TEE is the container. Skills are loaded code. Secrets are state.

products.name: Agent Wallet SDK
products.description: External developer SDK for AI agents with provable origin. Three calls: createAgentWallet() → recordInference() → settle(). Provider-agnostic — works with any AI API. Includes checkpoint/resume with anti-replay.

products.name: Morpheus Agent (Skill #1)
products.description: Morpheus decentralized inference integration. Runs as standalone MCP server (6 tools) or as a skill loaded into the TEE runtime. Birth certificates, signed action chains, session archiving, secure channel.

products.name: Secure Channel
products.description: Three-layer signed communication between AI agents and inference providers. Layer 1: agent signs prompts (working). Layer 2: provider signs responses (patch ready). Layer 3: ECDH encrypted channel with TEE attestation (designed).

products.name: Rootz Desktop V6
products.description: Electron desktop application providing TPM-sealed wallet keys, Secret/Note management on Polygon, ECDH encryption, IPFS upload, credit management, and MCP server for AI tool integration. The agent's Hardware Security Module.

team.name: Steven Sprague
team.background: Former CEO of Wave Systems Corp (2003-2017). Co-chaired TCG Technical Working Group. 25+ years in trusted computing, TPM, device attestation. Four US/international patents in device attestation and identity verification.

glossary.definition: A permanent on-chain record created when an AI agent is born, naming its two parents: the AI model that powers it and the human who authorized it.
Cryptographic evidence linking an AI output to a specific agent, model, and authorizer — signed, hash-linked, and blockchain-anchored.
An encrypted on-chain data wallet that serves as an AI agent's permanent identity and audit trail. Notes accumulate as the agent's chain of experience.
A Merkle root covering all actions in a session, written as a single on-chain Note. One transaction proves the integrity of the entire session.
A signed communication layer between agent and inference provider. Layer 1: agent signs. Layer 2: provider signs. Layer 3: ECDH encrypted.
Not storage — a messaging framework. Write to an address, all devices watching receive the update. Carries data, code, policy, instructions, and receipts.
Off-chain accumulation of signed actions with periodic on-chain settlement via Merkle root. Efficient: one transaction covers N operations.
Progressive trust model: Layer 1 (agent signs), Layer 2 (provider signs), Layer 3 (ECDH + TEE), Layer 4 (model provenance from signed sources).

partnerships.name: Morpheus
partnerships.description: Decentralized AI inference network. Provides the thinking — Rootz provides the proof.

standards.name: AI Discovery Standard
standards.description: Open standard for organizational AI identity discovery (/.well-known/ai)

standards.name: NIST AI Agent Security RFI
standards.description: Response submitted March 2026 on message security model for AI agents, organizational identity, and trusted computing standards application.
